凌晨1点。周五，特朗普对黑人乔治·弗洛伊德(George Floyd)死于一名白人警察手中后爆发的明尼阿波利斯骚乱做出回应。

这条推文仍然对美国公众开放，但必须在用户阅读警告标签后才能点击。

周五早上，白宫官方账号转发了特朗普总统的推文，称这是第二次美化暴力，推特上标记了这条推文。
这些暴徒是在玷污对乔治·弗洛伊德的记忆，我不会让这种事情发生的。刚刚与州长蒂姆·沃尔兹通话，告诉他军方一直与他同在。任何困难，我们都会承担控制权，但是，当抢劫开始时，射击就开始了。这条推文写道：谢谢你！
自从一名白人警察用膝盖按住黑人乔治·弗洛伊德的脖子直到他停止呼吸后，明尼苏达州一直处于混乱状态。
该公司在这条推文上贴上了警告标签，称其违反了规定。
推特对中国或激进的左翼民主党发布的所有谎言和宣传无动于衷。他们的目标是共和党人、保守党人和美国总统，总统写道。第230条应该由国会废除。在此之前，它将受到监管！
然后，他让白宫官方账号逐字转发了这条被标记的推文，鼓励这家社交媒体公司采取行动。
@Whitehouse的账号继续参与战斗，链接到伊朗最高领导人阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊(Ayatollah Ali Khamenei)上周发布的一条推文，该推文呼吁#对以色列进行圣战。
这条推文违反了推特关于美化暴力的规定。然而，推特已经决定，它将允许恐怖分子、独裁者和外国宣传者滥用其平台，白宫官方账号的一条推文写道。
在最初令人不快的特朗普推特上，推特在不到三个小时后对其发出警告，称其采取行动是为了防止其他人受到启发而实施暴力行为。Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)事先得到了通知。
这条推文不能再被点赞或回复，推特的算法也不会推荐这条推文，尽管仍有可能转发带评论的推文--特朗普的信息最初是隐藏的。
根据针对政府官员的特殊规定，仍有可能推翻警告信息，查看这条推文，这些规定保护了公众知道他们的政客们说了什么的权利。
最近几天，特朗普因对他的推文进行事实核查而与这家社交媒体网站发生争执。
同样在周五早上，他援引福克斯新闻玛丽亚·巴蒂罗莫(Maria Bartiromo)的话说，总统已经成为推特(Twitter)的目标。
特朗普接着补充道，亚当·希夫(Adam Schiff)和其他许多人关于俄罗斯猎巫计划(Witch Hunt Plus，Plus，Plus)的所有谎言和欺诈性声明是怎么回事？那中国的宣传呢？谁错了？没有旗帜吗？他问道。
然后，他引用了支持特朗普的福克斯新闻(Fox News)名人珍宁·皮罗(Jeanine Pirro)的话，后者也呼吁监管Twitter。
周四，总统签署了一项行政命令，寻求剥夺社交媒体巨头的法律保护，这可能会让他们面临大量诉讼-但支持与Twitter保持距离的Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)。
周二，推特给特朗普的两条推文贴上了事实核查的标签，这两条推文声称，2020年大选的邮寄投票将存在严重的舞弊行为。
这条消息是对最初一条推文的回复-而不是Twitter发出的危险信号-这条推文针对明尼阿波利斯民主党市长对危机的处理。
在特朗普发出这条消息的三个小时后，推特在这条推文上宣布了这条枪击事件的公共利益公告。
这家社交媒体巨头表示，特朗普的推文违反了我们关于美化暴力的政策，这是基于最后一句话的历史背景，它与暴力的联系，以及它今天可能引发类似行动的风险。
按照本通知的标准，与该推文的互动将受到限制。一份声明解释说，人们可以转发评论，但不能点赞、回复或转发。
然而，推特表示，考虑到这条推文与正在进行的重大公共事务的关联性，这条推文保持可访问是符合公众利益的。
该公司的政策表示，民选官员的推文不受通常规则的约束，因为知道并能够讨论他们的行动和声明符合重大公众利益。
因此，在极少数情况下，我们可能会选择留下一条民选官员或政府官员的推文，否则这些推文就会被删除。
这些豁免仅适用于粉丝超过10万的账户。特朗普拥有8000多万人。
该网站告诉Axios，Twitter内部的团队已经决定对总统的推文发出危险信号，首席执行官杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)在事情发生之前就得到了通知。
特朗普尚未对这条警告信息做出回应，这条警告信息出现在华盛顿凌晨4点左右。
总统对抢劫者发出威胁之前，明尼阿波利斯发生了第三晚的抗议活动，并伴随着全美各地的进一步集会。
数百名国民警卫队士兵被部署到明尼阿波利斯和邻近的圣保罗的街道上，市长弗雷宣布进入紧急状态，骚乱升级，城市各地的汽车和建筑被焚烧。
国民警卫队已经赶到现场。他们在明尼阿波利斯，做好了充分的准备。乔治·弗洛伊德不会白白牺牲。尊重他的记忆！&39；特朗普周五上午10点左右在推特上写错了场景。
明尼阿波利斯第三警局被纵火，由于担心天然气管道被切断后可能会爆炸，警察局被疏散。
弗雷称这种抢劫是不可接受的，但对特朗普拒绝为自己的行为负责感到愤怒，称总统对明尼阿波利斯的力量一无所知。
CNN记者奥马尔·希门尼斯是黑人，他在明尼阿波利斯进行实况拍摄时被警方逮捕，为种族愤怒火上浇油。
在纽约市，可以看到纽约警察局官员在地上与抗议者发生斗殴，该市至少有70人被捕。
俄亥俄州的抗议者砸碎了哥伦布市中心州议会的窗户，并突袭了大楼，而示威者则破坏了洛杉矶市中心的一辆警车。
在肯塔基州，路易斯维尔市中心的一场抗议活动中有7人被枪杀，抗议活动要求为3月份被警察枪杀的黑人女性布雷娜·泰勒伸张正义。在弗洛伊德死后，这起案件再次成为聚光灯下的焦点。
弗洛伊德现年46岁，周一去世，此前一名白人警察用膝盖将他按住，尽管弗洛伊德恳求他无法呼吸。
就在总统与推特(Twitter)争执升级的几个小时前，他签署了一项命令，寻求取消对社交媒体公司的法律保护，他指责社交媒体公司存在政治偏见。
这项命令可能会使Twitter、Facebook和谷歌面临诉讼，因为法律保护禁止它们对其平台上的帖子负责，并允许它们缓和内容。
特朗普的行政命令称，推特(Twitter)和脸书(Facebook)等网站即使不是史无前例的，也拥有巨大的权力来影响对公共事件的解读。推特表示，该命令是一项攻击言论自由的政治举动。
特朗普还赞扬了Facebook创始人马克·扎克伯格对推特对推特进行事实核查的决定的批评。Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格今天批评了推特，特朗普在分享扎克伯格的声明之前写道。
扎克伯格在接受福克斯新闻(Fox News)采访时表示：我认为，我们在这方面的政策与推特不同。
他补充说：我只是坚信，Facebook不应该成为人们在网上所说的一切真相的仲裁者。
私营公司可能不应该这样做，特别是这些平台公司，不应该处于这样做的位置。
民主党人指责扎克伯格在审查制度上的言论迎合总统，这样Facebook就不会成为攻击目标。
扎克伯格在福克斯新闻(Fox News)-一台向种族主义者和阴谋论者提供扩音器的仇恨营利机器-谈论社交媒体平台应该如何本质上允许政客撒谎而不承担后果。这正在侵蚀我们的民主，参议员伊丽莎白·沃伦(Elizabeth Warren)说。
众议院议长南希·佩洛西(Nancy Pelosi)措辞更严厉，称特朗普正在利用这场争执分散人们对冠状病毒爆发的注意力。
佩洛西在周四的新闻发布会上说，这是令人愤慨的，但这是一种令人愤慨的情况。虽然推特正在对总统关于投票的言论进行事实核查，但它仍然不会去掉总统在那里发表的歪曲。
她说，Facebook，所有这些公司，都是为了赚钱。他们的商业模式是以牺牲他们所知道的真相和事实为代价来赚钱。他们捍卫，他们捍卫这一点。
在强调了扎克伯格的声明后，特朗普问道：“推特上批评奥巴马的言论了吗？你可以保留你的博士学位吗？
总统在保证如果你喜欢你现有的医生，就可以根据“平价医疗法案”留住你的医生，而奥巴马当时正努力让该法案在国会获得通过。他指的是奥巴马的这项法案。“平价医疗法案”是指奥巴马在保证如果你喜欢你的医生，你可以根据“平价医疗法案”(Affordable Care Act)保留你的医生。
然而，这一承诺后来被放弃，奥巴马承认，一些人最终可能不得不更换医生。
在后来的一条推文中，特朗普还重申了他对邮寄选民欺诈的可疑说法，正是这些欺诈让他从一开始就被Twitter贴上了错误信息的标签。
特朗普声称，邮寄投票将导致大规模的欺诈和滥用，也会导致我们伟大的共和党的终结。邮寄投票是议程上的重要议题，因为在流感大流行期间亲自投票的风险很大。(注：邮寄投票是议程上的优先事项，因为在流感大流行期间亲自投票的风险很高)，这将导致大规模的欺诈和滥用行为，并导致我们伟大的共和党的终结。
推特没有像对他周二的推文那样对昨天的言论发出危险信号，这些推文现在带有一个警告标志和一个链接，可以获取邮寄选票的相关事实。
特朗普指责推特通过对他进行事实核查，成为一个有观点的编辑，而不是一个中立的平台。
总统还猛烈抨击了该公司的一名高管、用户诚信主管约尔·罗斯(Yoel Roth)，指控他在事实核查方面存在欺诈行为。推特表示，他没有参与其中。
当被问及他是否想要摆脱Twitter时，他说：如果它是合法的，如果它能够合法关闭，我会的。
特朗普在司法部长比尔·巴尔(Bill Barr)的注视下推出了强硬的语言，总统签署了一项命令，可能会让Twitter和其他社交媒体平台面临接二连三的诉讼。
巴尔说，当公司策划他们的收藏并参与审查时，内容公司就成了出版商，不应该有权得到法律保护。
坦率地说，我们今天在这里是为了捍卫言论自由，使其免受美国历史上所面临的最大危险之一的伤害，你和任何人一样清楚正在发生什么。这并不好，特朗普在签署这份订单前表示，就在几天前，推特首次提供了特朗普工作人员所说的对他自己的推文进行事实核查的服务。
当被问及他是否真的会寻求利用法院关闭推特时，特朗普回答说：我认为：如果推特不光彩，如果你要让这样一个人来担任你的法官和陪审团，我认为你已经关闭了推特，就我而言，他指的是推特的廉正负责人，他已经被曝光在推特上发布了强烈批评特朗普和共和党高层的推文。(注：推特是一名廉洁的负责人，据披露，他曾在推特上发布强烈批评特朗普和共和党高层的推文。)在被问及是否真的会寻求利用法院关闭推特时，特朗普回答说：如果推特不光彩，如果你要让这样的人来担任你的法官和陪审团，我认为你已经关闭了推特。
特朗普是一位亿万富翁，在竞选总统之前积累了一个品牌和房地产帝国，他补充道：少数强大的社交媒体垄断企业控制着美国很大一部分公共和私人交流，我们知道它们是什么，我们不一定要说出它们的名字，我们会给你一份完整的清单。(注：特朗普在竞选总统之前积累了一个品牌和房地产帝国)，他补充道：少数几家强大的社交媒体垄断企业控制着美国很大一部分公共和私人交流，我们知道它们是什么，我们不一定要说出它们的名字，我们会给你一个完整的清单。
他接着说：他们拥有不受约束的权力，可以谴责、限制、编辑、塑造、隐藏、改变普通公民或广大公众观众之间的几乎任何形式的交流。
特朗普在抨击推特时提到了2016年大选-如果公司试图在法庭上声称这一行动是出于政治动机，律师们可能会试图标记这一事实。
特朗普说：我们不能允许这种情况发生，特别是当他们在做他们正在做的事情的时候。因为他们做得不对。他们有自己的观点。如果按照这一点来衡量，2016年的成功确实令人惊叹，但我们不能让这种情况继续发生。这是非常、非常不公平的。
特朗普称，他们的所作所为等同于垄断，你可以这么说。这相当于接管了电视广播。不能让它发生。否则我们将不会有民主。我们不会与共和国有任何关系。
特朗普还试图利用联邦金库的力量作为压力，他说，我们不会进入任何压制言论自由的社交媒体公司。
他说，政府将花费数十亿美元给他们钱，并称这些公司足够富有，尽管独立账户显示政府总广告支出要低得多。
特朗普说：我们将不会做任何事情，或者只做很少的事情。
当被问及是否会考虑删除他的推特账户时，特朗普表示，如果我们在美国有一个公平的媒体，他会毫不犹豫地这么做。
特朗普签署的这项命令的措辞特别提到了推特，因为它决定标记总统的两条推文。该平台提供了更多信息，尽管它没有删除这些推文。
这似乎是在签署前广为流传的一份命令草案之后增加的。
命令称，Twitter现在有选择地决定在某些推文上贴上警告标签，这种方式明显反映了政治偏见。据报道，推特似乎从未给另一位政治家的推文贴上过这样的标签。就在上周，众议员亚当·希夫(Adam Schiff)还在继续通过兜售长期被证明是错误的俄罗斯串通恶作剧来误导他的追随者，Twitter没有标记这些推文。不出所料，负责所谓网站诚信的官员在自己的推文中炫耀自己的政治偏见。
希夫是众议院情报委员会的民主党主席，也是特朗普最喜欢的目标。
另一个章节挑出了四个科技平台--相比之下，一份订单草案没有提到Instagram和YouTube的名字。
它说，Twitter、Facebook、Instagram和YouTube拥有巨大(如果不是前所未有的)权力，可以影响对公共事件的解读，审查、删除或消失信息，以及控制人们看到或没有看到的内容。
还有人描述了一个新的政府工作组，该工作组将对媒体组织进行评估，其中包括评估有偏见的媒体组织被用来审查内容的证据。&#39；
根据适用法律，本订单第4(B)节中描述的投诉将与工作组共享。工作组还应收集关于以下方面的公开可用信息：(Iv)依赖第三方实体，包括承包商、组织和个人，并带有审查内容的偏见标记，它说。
至少有一个没有出现在公开可用的表格草案中的章节将新的权力置于巴尔之下。
关于政府在线广告支出的一节规定：司法部应审查本节(B)项所述报告中确定的每个在线平台基于观点的言论限制，并评估是否有任何在线平台因观点歧视、欺骗消费者或其他不良做法而成为政府言论的问题载体。
关于FCC的一节考虑了新增加的对冲。联邦贸易委员会应考虑编写一份报告，说明指控违反法律的投诉是否牵涉到其条款。一份草案说，该机构将制作这份报告-有效地让它退出实际制作一份报告。
其中一个部分让AG的工作组从财务角度调查一个领域，即平台的政策是否采取了限制具有特定观点的用户在该平台上赚钱的能力的方式，而不是与其他类似情况的用户相比。
关键的法律部分是特朗普向联邦通信委员会(FCC)发出的命令，要求其澄清如何执行通信体面法案第230条下的规定。
也就是说，联邦法律在很大程度上免除了在线平台对用户帖子的法律责任。
但该命令规定，如果公司从事编辑行为，这项保护不应适用-这意味着公司自己判断人们的观点是如何呈现的。
这就是特朗普指责Twitter通过对他进行事实核查所做的事情，以及保守派声称他们的帖子比那些持有更自由观点的帖子提升得更少的情况。
这样的举动可能会让Twitter、Facebook和其他平台面临雪崩般的诉讼，人们声称他们的观点受到了不公平的审查。
它还要求该机构调查社交媒体平台是否在使用欺骗性政策，通过不公开宣布如何决定如何处理观点来限制内容。
特朗普还建立了一种机制，允许美国人报告社交媒体巨头涉嫌的政治审查或偏见，联邦贸易委员会将对此进行调查。
白宫技术偏见报告工具将收集有关在线审查的投诉，并将其提交给司法部和联邦贸易委员会(FTC)。
这项命令也遭到了公民自由团体和商会的拒绝，商会通常是共和党政策的可靠支持者。
据“纽约时报”报道，美国商会一名官员在一份声明中表示，我们相信言论自由和从事商业的权利是美国自由企业制度的基础。
不管是什么情况导致了这一点，美国的公共政策都不是这样制定的。行政命令不能被恰当地用来改变经济状况。

Twitter flags Donald Trump's 'when the looting starts the shooting starts' threat for 'inciting violence' AGAIN after he repeats it on White House account amid furious attacks at platform for 'targeting' him
 President Trump lashed out at Twitter Friday morning for covering up his tweet with a warning label saying that it was 'glorifying violence'
 'They have targeted Republicans, Conservatives & the President of the United States,' the president complained
 At 1 a.m. Friday, Trump responded to the Minneapolis riots that broke out after the death of George Floyd, a black man, at the hands of a white police officer
 He labeled protesters 'THUGS' and said 'when the looting starts, the shootings starts,' as he threatened to take federal control of the city
 Several hours later, Twitter slapped a warning label over the tweet saying that it 'violated the Twitter Rules about glorifying violence'
 The tweet is still accessible to the American public, but it has to be clicked into after users read the warning label
 The move comes one day after the president signed an executive order, which was a direct response to Twitter fact-checking two of his tweets about voting
        Twitter flagged President Trump's tweet saying it was 'glorifying violence' for a second time after the official White House account reposted it Friday morning.
 'These THUGS are dishonoring the memory of George Floyd, and I won’t let that happen. Just spoke to Governor Tim Walz and told him that the Military is with him all the way. Any difficulty and we will assume control but, when the looting starts, the shooting starts. Thank you!' the tweet read.
 There's been chaos in the Minnesota city since a white police officer took the life of a black man, George Floyd, by kneeing him in the neck until he stopped breathing.
  The company attached a warning label to the tweet saying that it violated the rules.
  'Twitter is doing nothing about all of the lies & propaganda being put out by China or the Radical Left Democrat Party. They have targeted Republicans, Conservatives & the President of the United States,' the president wrote. 'Section 230 should be revoked by Congress. Until then, it will be regulated!'
 He then had  the official White House account repost the flagged tweet verbatim, daring the social media company to act.
             The @WhiteHouse's account continued to engage in the battle, by linking to a tweet sent out by Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, last week that called for #jihad against Israel.
 'This Tweet violated the Twitter Rules about glorifying violence. However, Twitter has determined that it will allow terrorists, dictators, and foreign propagandists to abuse its platform,' a tweet from the official White House account read.
 On the original offending Trump tweet, Twitter put a warning on it less than three hours later, saying it had 'taken action in the interest of preventing others from being inspired to commit violent acts'. Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey was informed in advance.
 The tweet can no longer be liked or replied to and will not be recommended by Twitter's algorithm, although retweets with comment are still possible - with Trump's message initially hidden.
 It is still possible to override the warning message and view the tweet, under special rules for government officials which protect the public's right to know what their politicians have said.
 Trump has feuded with the social media site in recent days for fact-checking his tweets.
 Also on Friday morning, he quoted Fox News' Maria Bartiromo saying, 'he President has been targeted by Twitter.'
 Trump then added, 'What about all of the lies and fraudulent statements made by Adam Schiff, and so many others, on the Russian Witch Hunt Plus, Plus, Plus? What about China’s propaganda? WHO’s mistakes? No flags?' he asked.
 He then quoted Jeanine Pirro, a pro-Trump Fox News personality, who also called for regulating Twitter.
 The president had signed an executive order Thursday seeking to strip social media giants of their legal protections, potentially exposing them to a flood of lawsuits- but backed Facebook chief Mark Zuckerberg who distanced himself from Twitter.
 Twitter had put a fact-checking label on two of Trump's tweets on Tuesday which claimed that mail-in voting in the 2020 election would be 'substantially fraudulent.'
  That message was a reply to an initial tweet - not red-flagged by Twitter - which took aim at the Democratic mayor of Minneapolis for his handling of the crisis.
 Twitter announced the 'public interest notice' on the 'shooting' tweet around three hours after Trump had sent the message.
 The social media giant said Trump's tweet 'violates our policies regarding the glorification of violence based on the historical context of the last line, its connection to violence, and the risk it could inspire similar actions today'.
 'As is standard with this notice, engagements with the tweet will be limited. People will be able to retweet with comment, but will not be able to like, reply or retweet it,' a statement explained.
 However, Twitter said it is 'in the public interest for the tweet to remain accessible' given its 'relevance to ongoing matters of public importance'.
 Tweets from elected officials are exempt from the usual rules because there is a 'significant public interest in knowing and being able to discuss their actions and statements', the company's policies say.
 'As a result, in rare instances, we may choose to leave up a Tweet from an elected or government official that would otherwise be taken down.'
 These exemptions are only available to accounts with more than 100,000 followers. Trump has more than 80million.
 The website told  Axios that 'teams within Twitter' had decided to red-flag the president's tweet and that CEO Jack Dorsey was informed before it happened.
 Trump has yet to respond to the warning message, which appeared at around 4am in Washington.
 The president's threat to looters came after a third night of protests in Minneapolis accompanied by further rallies around the United States.
 Hundreds of National Guard soldiers were deployed to the streets of Minneapolis and neighboring St. Paul and Mayor Frey declared a state of emergency as rioting escalated and cars and buildings were torched across the city.
 'The National Guard has arrived on the scene. They are in Minneapolis and fully prepared. George Floyd will not have died in vain. Respect his memory!!!' Trump tweeted mid-morning Friday. He misspelled scene.
     Minneapolis's Third Precinct police station was set on fire and evacuated amid fears it could explode after gas lines were cut.
 Frey called the looting 'unacceptable' but raged at Trump for 'refusing to take responsibility for your own actions,' saying the president 'knows nothing about the strength of Minneapolis.'
 CNN reporter Omar Jimenez, who is black, was arrested by police in Minneapolis while doing a live-shot, adding more fuel to the racial outrage.
 In New York City, NYPD officers were seen brawling on the ground with protesters as at least 70 people were arrested in the city.
 Protesters in Ohio smashed the windows of the statehouse in downtown Columbus and raided the building, while demonstrators damaged a police cruiser in downtown Los Angeles.
 In Kentucky, seven people were shot in downtown Louisville during a protest demanding justice for black woman Breonna Taylor who was shot dead by cops in March - a case which has returned to the spotlight after Floyd's death.
 Floyd, 46, died on Monday after a white police officer pinned him down with his knee despite Floyd pleading that he could not breathe.
   The latest escalation in the president's row with Twitter comes just hours after he signed an order seeking to scrap legal protections for social media firms, which he accuses of political bias.
 The order could open Twitter, Facebook and Google up to lawsuits by diluting the legal protection which stops them from being liable for posts on their platforms, and which also allows them to moderate content.
 Trump's executive order said websites such as Twitter and Facebook 'wield immense, if not unprecedented, power to shape the interpretation of public events'. Twitter said the order was a political move which attacked free speech.
 Trump also praised Facebook founder Mark Zuckerberg for his criticism of Twitter's decision to fact-check the tweets. 'CEO Mark Zuckerberg is today criticizing Twitter,' Trump wrote before sharing Zuckerberg's statement.
 'We have a different policy than, I think, Twitter on this,' Zuckerberg said in an interview with  Fox News.
 'I just believe strongly that Facebook shouldn't be the arbiter of truth of everything that people say online,' he added.
 'Private companies probably shouldn't be, especially these platform companies, shouldn't be in the position of doing that.'
 Zuckerberg has been accused by Democrats of pandering to the President with his comments about censorship so that Facebook will not be targeted.
 'Zuckerberg went on Fox News—a hate-for-profit machine that gives a megaphone to racists and conspiracy theorists—to talk about how social media platforms should essentially allow politicians to lie without consequences. This is eroding our democracy,' said Senator Elizabeth Warren.
 House Speaker Nancy Pelosi hit harder saying that Trump was using the feud as a distraction from the coronavirus outbreak.
  'It's outrageous, but it's an outrageous situation,' Pelosi said at a Thursday press conference. 'While Twitter is putting up its fact-check under what the president says about voting, it still won't take off the misrepresentations the president's putting out there.'
 'Facebook, all of them, they're all about making money,' she said. 'Their business model is to make money at the expense of the truth and the facts that they know. And they defend, they defend that.'
 After highlighting Zuckerberg's statement, Trump asked: 'Did Twitter criticize Obama for his 'you can keep your Dr''?'
 The president was referring to Obama's during assurance that 'if you like the doctor you have, you can keep your doctor' under the Affordable Care Act which he was struggling to steer through Congress at the time.
 However, this promise was later abandoned and Obama conceded that some people 'might end up having to switch doctors'.
 In a later tweet, Trump also renewed his dubious claims about mail-in voter fraud that had earned him the misinformation tag from Twitter in the first place.
 Trump claimed that mail-in voting - which is high on the agenda because of the risks of voting in person during the pandemic - would lead to 'massive fraud and abuse' as well as the 'end of our great Republican Party'.
 Twitter did not red-flag yesterday's comments as it had done for his Tuesday tweets, which now appear with a warning sign and a link to 'get the facts about mail-in ballots'.
     Trump accused Twitter of becoming an 'editor with a point of view' and not a 'neutral platform' by fact-checking him.
 The president also  slammed one of its executives, Yoel Roth, its head of user integrity, accusing him of 'fraud' for the fact check. Twitter says he was not involved in it.
 Asked if he wanted to get rid of Twitter he said: 'If it was legal, if it was able to be legally shut down, I would.'
 Trump rolled out the tough language as Attorney General Bill Barr looked on and the president signed an order that could expose Twitter and other social media platforms to a barrage of lawsuits.
 Barr said when firms 'curate' their collection and engage in 'censoring' content firms become 'publishers' and shouldn't be entitled to a legal 'shield.'
  'We're here today to defend free speech from one of the greatest dangers it has faced in American history, frankly, and you know what's going on as well as anybody. It's not good,' Trump said before inking the order, which came just days after Twitter for the first time provided what Trump's staff calls a fact-check on his own tweets.
 Pressed on whether he would in fact seek to use the courts to shut down Twitter, Trump responded: 'I think this: if Twitter were not honorable, if you're going to have a guy like this be your judge and jury I think you shut it down as far as I'm concerned,' in reference to Twitter's 'head of integrity,' who has been revealed to have posted tweets highly critical of Trump and top Republicans.
 Trump, a billionaire who amassed a branding and real estate empire before running for president, added: 'A small handful of powerful social media monopolies controls a vast portion of all public and private communication in the United States and we know what they are, we don't have to name them, we're going to give you a complete listing.'
 He continued: 'They've had unchecked power to censure, restrict, edit, shape, hide, alter virtually any form of communication between private citizens or large public audiences.'
 Trump brought up the 2016 elections as he tore into Twitter – a fact lawyers might try to flag if companies try to claim in court the action is politically motivated.
 'We can't allow that to happen especially when they go about doing what they're doing,' Trump said. 'Because they're doing things incorrectly. They have points of view. And if we go by that it's actually amazing that there was a success in 2016, but we can't let this continue to happen. It's very, very unfair.'
 'What they're doing is tantamount to monopoly you can say,' Trump claimed. It's tantamount to taking over the airwaves. Can't let it happen. Otherwise we're not going to have a democracy. We're not going to have anything to do with a republic.'
 Trump also tried to use the power of federal purse strings as pressure, saying we 'are not going in any social media company that repress[es] free speech.'
 He said the government  spends 'billions of dollars on giving them money' and called the firms 'rich enough,' although independent accounts put the total government ad spending far lower.
 'We're going to be doing none of it or very little of it,' Trump said.
 Asked if he would consider simply deleting his Twitter account given his concerns, Trump said he would 'do that in a heartbeat' if we had a 'fair press' in the U.S.
 The language of the order Trump signed specifically singled out Twitter for its decision to flag two of the president's tweets. The platform provided additional information, although it did not take down the tweets.
 It appeared to have been added since a draft order which was circulated widely in the run-up to the signing.
 'Twitter now selectively decides to place a warning label on certain tweets in a manner that clearly reflects political bias,' said the order. 'As has been reported, Twitter seems never to have placed such a label on another politician's tweet. As recently as last week, Representative Adam Schiff was continuing to mislead his followers by peddling the long-disproved Russian Collusion Hoax, and Twitter did not flag those tweets. Unsurprisingly, its officer in charge of so-called 'Site Integrity' has flaunted his political bias in his own tweets.'
 Schiff is the Democratic chair of the House Intelligence Committee and a favorite Trump target.
 Another section singled out four tech platforms - compared to a draft order which did not name Instagram and YouTube.
 'Twitter, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube wield immense, if not unprecedented, power to shape the interpretation of public events; to censor, delete, or disappear information; and to control what people see or do not see,' it said.
 Still another describes a new government 'working group' that will, among other things, evaluate media organizations with evidence of 'bias' being used to review content. '
 'Complaints described in section 4(b) of this order will be shared with the working group, consistent with applicable law. The working group shall also collect publicly available information regarding the following: (iv) reliance on third-party entities, including contractors, organizations, and individuals, with indicia of bias to review content,' it says.
 At least one section that did not appear in a draft form publicly available put new authority under Barr.
 A section on government expenditures on online ads states: 'The Department of Justice shall review the viewpoint-based speech restrictions imposed by each online platform identified in the report described in subsection (b) of this section and assess whether any online platforms are problematic vehicles for government speech due to viewpoint discrimination, deception to consumers, or other bad practices.'
 A section on the FCC considers a newly-added hedge. 'The FTC shall consider developing a report' on whether complaints alleging a violation of the law implicate its provisions. A draft said the agency would produce the report - effectively giving it an exit from actually producing one.
 One section has the AG's working group probe an area with a financial angle, whether the platforms' policies act in ways 'that limit the ability of users with particular viewpoints to earn money on the platform compared with other users similarly situated.'
 The crucial legal section is Trump's order to the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to clarify how to enforce regulations under Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act.
 That is the federal law largely exempting online platforms from legal liability for users' posts.
 But the order says that the protection should not apply if companies are 'engaged in editorial conduct' - meaning making a judgment for themselves about how people's points of view are presented.
 That is what Trump accuses Twitter of doing by fact-checking him, and what conservatives claim happens to their posts which are promoted less than those with more liberal viewpoints.
 Such a move could open up Twitter, Facebook and other platforms to an avalanche of lawsuits from people claiming their views have been unfairly censored.
 It also requires the agency to look at whether social media platforms are using 'deceptive' policies to moderate content by not openly declaring how they decide how viewpoints are dealt with.
 Trump also set up a mechanism allowing Americans to report alleged political censorship or bias by the social media giants which will be investigated by the Federal Trade Commission.
 The White House tech bias reporting tool will collect complaints of online censorship and submit them to the Department of Justice and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC).
  The order was also rejected by civil liberties groups - and by the Chamber of Commerce, normally a reliable supporter of Republican policy.
  'We believe that free speech and the right to engage in commerce are foundational to the American free enterprise system,' a Chamber official said in a statement, the New York Times reported.
 'Regardless of the circumstances that led up to this, this is not how public policy is made in the United States. An executive order cannot be properly used to change fe

韩国200多所学校在重新开学几天后就被迫关闭，原因是病毒病例出现了新的激增。
周三早些时候，随着国家开始放松病毒限制，数千名学生已经返回学校。
但就在一天后，又记录了79例新病例，这是两个月来最高的单日数字。
这个仓库位于富川市，由该国最大的电子商务公司Coupang运营，官员们表示，该设施没有严格遵守感染控制措施。卫生官员甚至在工人的鞋子和衣服上发现了新冠肺炎的痕迹。
随着韩国继续测试该中心的数千名员工，案件数量可能会继续上升。
周五记录了约58例新病例，使全国病例总数达到11402例。
目前，富川共有251所学校被迫关闭。“韩国时报”的一篇报道援引教育部的话说，首都首尔还有117所学校也推迟了重新开学。
首尔的一名学生被发现感染了这种病毒，她的母亲在Coupang仓库工作。
首尔及其周边城市的公园和博物馆现在已经关闭，企业被敦促鼓励更灵活的工作，人们再次被要求避免大规模集会。
今年早些时候，韩国经历了该病毒最严重的爆发之一，但由于一项广泛的追踪、检测和治疗计划，它似乎已得到控制。
然而，最近几周出现了新的聚居区，其中一个与本月早些时候一名男子光顾首尔梨泰院夜生活区的五家夜总会和酒吧有关。
note_en:
More than 200 schools in South Korea have been forced to close just days after they re-opened, due to a new spike in virus cases.
 Thousands of students had earlier on Wednesday returned to school as the country began easing virus restrictions.
 But just a day later, 79 new cases were recorded, the highest daily figure in two months.
  The warehouse, in the city of Bucheon, is run by the country's biggest e-commerce firm Coupang, and officials have said the facility was not strictly complying with infection control measures. Health officials even discovered traces of Covid-19 on workers' shoes and clothes.
   It's possible that South Korea will continue to see a rise in cases as it continues to test thousands of employees from the centre.
 Some 58 new cases were recorded on Friday, bringing the total number of cases nationwide to 11,402.
  A total of 251 schools in Bucheon have now been forced to close.  A report by the Korea Times, quoting the Ministry of Education, says a further 117 schools in the capital Seoul have also postponed their re-opening.
 One student in Seoul, whose mother worked at the Coupang warehouse, had been found to have the virus.
  Public parks and museums across Seoul and its surrounding cities have now been closed, businesses are being urged to encourage more flexible working, and people are once again being asked to avoid mass gatherings.
 South Korea endured one of the worst outbreaks of the virus earlier this year, but had appeared to have brought it under control, thanks to an extensive "trace, test and treat" programme.
 Recent weeks, however, have seen new clusters emerge, including one linked to a man who visited five nightclubs and bars in Seoul's nightlife district of Itaewon earlier this month.
title: 冠状病毒：韩国在经历数周来最大涨幅后再次关闭学校
title_en: Coronavirus: South Korea closes schools again after biggest spike in weeks

美国总统唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)周五宣布，美国将切断与世界卫生组织(World Health Organization)的联系。
他声称世卫组织是以中国为中心的，并指责该机构在疫情爆发早期建议不要对中国实施旅行禁令。
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)周五宣布，美国将切断与世界卫生组织(World Health Organization)的联系。
特朗普在白宫玫瑰园举行的新闻发布会上表示，尽管与美国每年支付的约4.5亿美元相比，中国每年只需支付4000万美元，但中国完全控制了世界卫生组织。
世界需要中国在病毒问题上给出答案。我们必须有透明度。他补充说，为什么中国要将武汉的感染者全部封锁到中国所有其他地区？他补充说。它没有去北京，没有去其他任何地方，但他们允许他们自由地在世界各地旅行，包括欧洲和美国。
特朗普多次批评世卫组织对冠状病毒的反应，冠状病毒对美国的打击比其他任何国家都严重，目前他的政府对疫情的反应受到了密切关注。他声称世卫组织是以中国为中心的，并指责该机构在疫情爆发早期建议不要对中国实施旅行禁令。
幸运的是，我没有被说服，并暂停了从中国旅行，拯救了无数人的生命，特朗普在4月14日表示。
本月早些时候，特朗普威胁要永久切断美国对世卫组织的资助。他在一封信中表示，如果世卫组织不承诺在未来30天内做出重大实质性改善，我将永久冻结美国对世界卫生组织的资助，并重新考虑我们在该组织的成员资格。
周五，特朗普表示，世卫组织未能进行所要求的、亟需的改革，美国将于今天终止我们与世界卫生组织的关系，并将这些资金重新用于世界范围内其他亟需的全球公共卫生需求。
世卫组织的资金以两年预算周期运行。世卫组织发言人塔里克·贾萨雷维奇(Tarik Jasarevic)说，在2018年和2019年的资助周期，美国支付了2.37亿美元的必需评估以及6.56亿美元的自愿捐款，平均每年4.46亿美元，约占其总预算的14.67%。
5月20日，世卫组织官员表示，他们担心如果总统永久性地从这个国际机构撤出美国的资金，该机构的紧急项目将受到影响。
世卫组织卫生紧急情况项目执行主任迈克·瑞安博士当时表示，来自美国的大部分资金直接用于帮助处于各种脆弱和困难环境中的国家的项目。
瑞安说：我们显然必须与其他合作伙伴合作，以确保这些资金仍能流动。这将是向世界上一些最脆弱的人提供基本医疗服务的重大影响，我们相信，如果有必要，发达的捐赠者将介入填补这一缺口。
该机构为其对冠状病毒大流行的初步反应进行了辩护，称这给了世界各国领导人足够的时间在疫情爆发早期进行干预。
世卫组织从1月中旬开始对中国的疫情敲响警钟。3月11日，世卫组织官员宣布疫情为大流行，当时全球仅有12.1万例病例。根据约翰·霍普金斯大学(Johns Hopkins University)汇编的数据，目前全球已有超过580万人感染了这种病毒。
note_en:
President Donald Trump announced Friday that the United States will cut ties with the World Health Organization.
  He has claimed WHO is "China-centric" and blames the agency for advising against China travel bans early in the outbreak.
 President Donald Trump announced Friday that the United States will cut ties with the World Health Organization.
 "China has total control over the World Health Organization despite only paying $40 million per year compared to what the United States has been paying, which is approximately $450 million a year," Trump said during a press conference from the White House Rose Garden.
  "The world needs answers from China on the virus. We must have transparency. Why is it that China shut off infected people from Wuhan to all other parts of China?" he added. "It didn't go to Beijing, it went nowhere else, but they allowed them to freely travel throughout the world, including Europe and the United States."
 Trump has repeatedly criticized the WHO's response to the coronavirus,  which has hit the U.S. worse than any other country, amid scrutiny of his own administration's response to the pandemic. He has claimed WHO is "China-centric" and blames the agency for advising against China travel bans early in the outbreak.
 "Fortunately, I was not convinced and suspended travel from China saving untold numbers of lives," Trump said on April 14.
 Earlier this month, Trump threatened to  permanently cut off U.S. funding of the WHO. In a letter, he said that if the WHO "does not commit to major substantive improvements within the next 30 days, I will make my temporary freeze of United States funding to the World Health Organization permanent and reconsider our membership in the organization."
 On Friday, Trump said WHO "failed to make the requested greatly needed reform" and the U.S. "will be today terminating our relationship with the World Health Organization and redirecting those funds to other worldwide and deserving urgent global public health needs."
  The WHO's funding runs in two-year budget cycles. For the 2018 and 2019 funding cycle, the U.S. paid a $237 million required assessment as well as $656 million in voluntary contributions, averaging $446 million a year and representing about 14.67% of its total budget, according to WHO spokesman Tarik Jasarevic.
 On May 20, WHO officials said they worried the agency's emergency programs would suffer if the president permanently pulled U.S. funding from the international agency.
 Most funding from the United States goes directly out to the program that helps countries in "all sorts of fragile and difficult settings," Dr. Mike Ryan, executive director of WHO's health emergencies program, said at the time.
 "We'll obviously have to work with other partners to ensure those funds can still flow," Ryan said. "This is going to be a major implication for delivering essential health services to some of the most vulnerable people in the world, and we trust developed donors will, if necessary, step in to fill that gap."
 The agency has defended its initial response to the coronavirus pandemic,  saying it gave world leaders enough time to intervene early in the outbreak.
 The WHO started sounding the alarm on the outbreak in China in mid-January. On March 11, WHO officials  declared the outbreak a pandemic, when there were just 121,000 global cases. The virus has now infected more than 5.8 million people worldwide, according to data compiled by Johns Hopkins University.

note:
(TMU)-到目前为止，大多数人都知道本世纪初美国国家安全局(NSA)滥用监控的行为，但一本关于爱德华·斯诺登(Edward Snowden)的新书表明，通过之前的举报人和披露介绍给我们的元数据收集计划是“美国实时、不断更新的社会图谱”的一部分，这一图谱正在进行，而且比我们之前想象的要广泛得多。
这一消息来自记者巴顿·盖尔曼，他描述了他的新书“暗镜：爱德华·斯诺登和”连线“的美国监控国”(Dark Mirror：Edward Snowden and the American SurveMonitoring State for Wire)的内容。这篇题为“美国国家安全局绘制社交网络的秘密工具内幕”的文章列举了盖尔曼试图披露斯诺登首次向世界披露的项目的更多细节。
他说，他的发现震惊了他，并代表着对美国公民持续的生存威胁。
盖尔曼说，最初他想更多地了解美国国家安全局电话记录的后勤工作。斯诺登的档案暗示了该机构项目管道的细节，但没有解释。
数据收集的主要渠道“星风”(Stellarwind)是副总统迪克·切尼(Dick Cheney)在9·11事件几周后启动的一个国内监控项目。他要求所有特工和下属对FISA法院法官和国会隐瞒该项目，并在该项目上盖上最隐蔽的政府分类--ECI，意为“异常受控的信息”。
STELLARWIND促成了美国国家安全局备受推崇的社交网络地图工具“Mainway”的使用，该工具将AT&amp；T和Verizon等电话数据公司征召到特殊来源运营公司(Special Source Operations)谈判的秘密数据收集合同中，这些合同在经济上是有利可图的。
Mainway计划编纂了两个重要的，但(到目前为止)模糊的监视和数据挖掘目标：联系链和预计算。
虽然NSA长期以来一直坚称，他们的监控项目只是存储了无法追踪的元数据，可以帮助调查已知恐怖分子特工的活动，但我们现在知道，该机构正在积极利用和利用这些数据，以构建几乎令人难以置信的复杂的下一代社会图。正如盖尔曼所描述的，这个工具结合了“六度分离”(如果你愿意，也可以是凯文·培根的六度)和新冠肺炎之前的接触追踪模型。
一套新的软件工具被称为“联系人链”，在追捕和调查波士顿爆炸案袭击者焦哈尔·萨纳耶夫(Dzhokhar Tsarnaev)期间首次部署。这套软件工具使用了NSA截获的通信(即我们的个人信息)，包括语音、视频、电子邮件和聊天文本、附件、寻呼机消息等，以构建一种尖端的数据分析形式，可以通过算法解析数据记录，以说明任何和所有情报资产(READ、US)之间的间接关系。
根据盖尔曼的说法，Mainway变成了“国内外元数据的女王”，旨在找到内容没有透露…的模式。[以及]识别、跟踪、存储、操作和更新关系“，以创建一个全球图形，一个集成的图形地图，代表地球上几乎每个人的”行动和通信“。
这个工具被命名为“大令人敬畏的图表”，或者简称为“袋子”，它是“大型访问开发”伞形指令中的主要数据收集工具。它“将通话记录映射为网格上的”节点“和”边缘“，网格如此之大，以至于人类的大脑在没有辅助的情况下无法将其包含在内。”
美国国家安全局的Mainway计划试图使用其新的、有点仓促组装的软件来持续创建所有全球公民的“联系人链”个人资料。联邦调查局每天从电话公司征用超过10亿条新记录，国家安全局吸收这些信息是为了“抢占每个人的先机”。
这个想法被称为预计算，过去和现在都是为了创建一个“常量、复杂的…。7x24…。每个美国公民以及大量非公民和国际公民的实时、不断更新的社交图，被称为“记忆中的图表”(Graph-in-Memory)。
“所有的秘密--社会、医疗、政治、职业--都是预先计算出来的，全天候…。一个预先配置好的数据库，只需按一下按钮就能绘制出任何人的生活地图。“。
他坚持认为，只有22名高级官员有权下令建立所谓的联系人链。然而，这种权力的危险比比皆是。
在后911冲刺“主导全球通信基础设施”的过程中，美国国家安全局打开了名副其实的潘多拉魔盒，在这个盒子里，“各级政府(可能)以最强硬的手段，有时甚至是非法的手段，来监控因贫困、种族、宗教、族裔和移民身份而处于不利地位的社区。”
盖尔曼观察到，“在发达国家，几乎任何人都可以与计算机数据库中的至少一个事实联系起来，对手可以利用这些事实进行勒索、歧视、骚扰、金融或身份盗窃。”
“新发明的潜在力量，”盖尔曼写道，“无论起初多么令人厌恶，都不会永远潜伏在政府的军火库里。”
换句话说，如果你担心新冠肺炎时代的接触者追踪问题，那就不用再担心了：那艘船已经扬帆很久了。
note_en:
( TMU) – Most people know by now about the surveillance abuses perpetrated by the NSA earlier this century, but a new book about Edward Snowden suggests that the metadata collection programs introduced to us through previous whistleblowers and disclosures are part of a  “live, ever-updating social graph of the US” that is ongoing and far vaster than we previously imagined.
  The revelations come from journalist Barton Gellman, who described the content of his new book  Dark Mirror: Edward Snowden and the American Surveillance State for   Wired. The article, entitled  “Inside the NSA’s Secret Tool for Mapping Your Social Network,” catalogs Gellman’s attempts to reveal more details about the programs Snowden first disclosed to the world.
 What he found shocked him, and represents an ongoing existential threat to American citizens, he says.
  Gellman says that originally he wanted to understand more about the logistics of the NSA phone records. The Snowden archive hints at but does not explain the details of the agency’s project pipelines.
 The main thoroughfare of data collection, ‘ Stellarwind’, was a domestic surveillance program launched by Vice President Dick Cheney only weeks after 9/11. He mandated that all operatives and subordinates conceal the program from FISA Court judges and Congress, stamping it with the most covert of government classifications, ECI, which stands for,  “exceptionally controlled information.”
 Stellarwind facilitated ‘ Mainway’, the NSA’s prized social network mapping tool which conscripted telephone data companies like AT&T and Verizon into secretive–and financially lucrative–data collection contracts negotiated by Special Source Operations.
   The Mainway program codified two important, but (until now), obfuscated surveillance and data mining objectives:   contact chaining and   precomputation.
  While the NSA long maintained that their surveillance programs merely stored untraced metadata that could help investigate the activities of known terrorist operatives, we now know that the agency was actively leveraging and exploiting the data to build an almost mind-bogglingly complex, next-generation social graph. As described by Gellman, this tool combined the concepts of   “six degrees of separation” (or six degrees of Kevin Bacon, if you prefer) and a pre-COVID-19 model of contact tracing.
  Termed ‘ contact chaining’ and first deployed during the manhunt and investigation of Boston bomber Dzhokhar Tsarnaev, a new suite of software tools used the NSA’s intercepted communications ( read: our personal information), including voice, video, email and chat text, attachments, pager messages, etc. to build a cutting-edge form of data analysis that can algorithmically parse data records to illustrate indirect relationships between any and all intelligence assets ( read, us).
 According to Gellman, Mainway turned into  “the queen of metadata, foreign and domestic, designed to find patterns that content did not reveal…[and] identify, track, store, manipulate and update relationships” to create a global graph, an integrated graphical map, representing the  “movements and communications” of virtually everyone on Earth.
 Named  “the Big Awesome Graph,” or  “the BAG” for short, this tool was the principal data harvesting tool in the umbrella directive of  “Large Access Exploitation.” It  “mapped the call records as “nodes” and “edges” on a grid so large that the human mind, unaided, could not encompass it.”
   The NSA’s Mainway program sought to use its newly and somewhat hastily assembled software to continuously create ‘ contact chain’ profiles on all global citizens. The FBI commandeered over a billion new records each day from the telephone companies and the NSA ingested that info to   “get a head start on everyone.”
 Termed  pre-computation, the idea was and is to create a  “constant, complex…7×24… live, ever-updating social graph,” called ‘Graph-in-Memory’, of every US citizen and a large number of non-citizens and international citizens.
   “All kinds of secrets—social, medical, political, professional—were precomputed, 24/7…a database that was preconfigured to map anyone’s life at the touch of a button.”
 He maintains that only 22 top officials had the authority to order a so-called contact chain. However, the dangers of such power abound.
 In its post-911 sprint to  “  to dominate the global communications infrastructure,” the NSA opened a veritable Pandora’s box, whereby  “governments at all levels [may use] the power of the state most heavy-handedly, sometimes illegally, to monitor communities  disadvantaged by poverty, race, religion, ethnicity, and immigration status.”
 Gellman  observes that  “nearly anyone in the developed world can be linked to at least one fact in a computer database that an adversary could use for blackmail, discrimination, harassment, or financial or identity theft.”
 “The latent power of new inventions,” Gellman writes,  “no matter how repellent at first, does not lie forever dormant in government armories.”
  In other words, if you’re worried about contact tracing in the age of Covid-19, worry no more: that ship has long sailed.

这起诉讼由哥伦比亚大学骑士第一修正案研究所(Knight First Amendment Institute)、布伦南司法中心(Brennan Center For Justice)和辛普森·萨切尔·巴特利特律师事务所(Simpson Thacher；Bartlett)提起，旨在撤销国务院关于签证申请者在获得美国签证前必须披露其社交媒体账号的要求，以及有关保留和传播这些记录的相关规定。
去年，美国国务院开始要求签证申请人提供他们现在和以前的社交媒体用户名，这一举措每年影响到数百万申请前往美国旅行的非公民。这一规则变化是特朗普政府扩大其“增强型”筛查协议的努力的一部分。当时有报道称，如果国务院确定“需要这些信息来确认身份或进行更严格的国家安全审查”，就会使用这些信息。
在一份支持这起诉讼的文件中，Twitter和Reddit都表示，社交媒体政策“毫无疑问地冷却了大量言论”，这些规定违反了第一修正案“匿名发言和私下交往”的权利。
Twitter和Reddit总共拥有超过5.6亿用户，他们表示，他们的用户-其中许多人在他们的平台上没有使用真实姓名-被迫“为了去美国旅行而放弃匿名”，这“违反了第一修正案匿名发言和私下交往的权利”。
简报称：“Twitter和Reddit大力捍卫在其平台上为人们匿名发声的权利，匿名个人在这些平台上进行相应的交流，期望在没有具体表现出迫切需求的情况下，他们的身份不会被披露。”
“这种期望让思想自由交流在这些平台上蓬勃发展。”
Twitter美洲政策负责人杰西卡·赫雷拉-弗拉尼根(Jessica Herrera-Flanigan)表示，社交媒体规则“侵犯了这两项权利，我们很自豪能在这些关键的法律问题上给予支持。”Reddit的总法律顾问本·李(Ben Lee)称这项规定是政府“侵入性的越权”。
目前还不清楚有多少签证申请者(如果有的话)因为他们的社交媒体内容而被拒签。但自从社交媒体规则生效以来，批准的签证持有者拒绝进入美国发布其他人的社交媒体帖子的案例层出不穷。当时17岁的哈佛大学大一新生伊斯梅尔·阿贾维(Ismail Ajjawi)在波士顿洛根国际机场(Boston Logan International Airport)被拒之门外，此前美国边境官员搜查了他的手机，原因是他在社交媒体上发布了关于阿贾维朋友的帖子-而不是他自己的帖子。
美国-阿拉伯反歧视委员会的法律和政策主任Abed Ayoub当时告诉TechCrunch，Ajjawi的案件并不是孤立的。一周后，TechCrunch了解到另一名男子因一位远房熟人发送的WhatsApp消息而被拒绝进入美国。
note_en:
The lawsuit, brought by the Knight First Amendment Institute at  Columbia University,   the Brennan Center for Justice and law firm Simpson Thacher & Bartlett, seeks to undo both the State Department’s requirement that visa applicants must disclose their social media handles prior to obtaining a U.S. visa, as well as related rules over the retention and dissemination of those records.
 Last year, the State Department  began asking visa applicants for their current and former social media usernames, a move that affects millions of non-citizens applying to travel to the United States each year. The rule change was part of the Trump administration’s effort to expand its “enhanced” screening protocols. At the time, it was reported that the information would be used if the State Department determines that “such information is required to confirm identity or conduct more rigorous national security vetting.”
 In a filing supporting the lawsuit, both Twitter and Reddit said the social media policies “unquestionably chill a vast quantity of speech” and that the rules violate the First Amendment rights “to speak anonymously and associate privately.”
 Twitter and Reddit, which collectively have more than 560 million users, said their users — many of which don’t use their real names on their platforms — are forced to “surrender their anonymity in order to travel to the United States,” which “violates the First Amendment rights to speak anonymously and associate privately.”
 “Twitter and Reddit vigorously guard the right to speak anonymously for people on their platforms, and anonymous individuals correspondingly communicate on these platforms with the expectation that their identities will not be revealed without a specific showing of compelling need,”  the brief said.
 “That expectation allows the free exchange of ideas to flourish on these platforms.”
 Jessica Herrera-Flanigan, Twitter’s policy chief for the Americas, said the social media rule “infringes both of those rights and we are proud to lend our support on these critical legal issues.” Reddit’s general counsel Ben Lee called the rule an “intrusive overreach” by the government.
 It’s not known how many, if any, visa applicants have been denied a visa because of their social media content. But since the social media rule went into effect, cases emerged of approved visa holders  denied entry to the U.S. for other people’s social media postings. Ismail Ajjawi, a then 17-year-old freshman at Harvard University, was turned away at Boston Logan International Airport after U.S. border officials searched his phone after taking issue with social media postings of Ajjawi’s friends — and not his own.
 Abed Ayoub, legal and policy director at the American-Arab Anti-Discrimination Committee, told TechCrunch at the time that Ajjawi’s case was not isolated. A week later, TechCrunch learned of  another man who was denied entry to the U.S. because of a WhatsApp message sent by a distant acquaintance.

任何一位阅读这本书的读者，如果能指出他在书中碰巧注意到的任何错误或印刷错误，或任何他认为表达不清楚的段落，我都将不胜感激。
我手头有一批第二部分和第三部分的女士，希望能够--如果我的生命、健康和机会被允许的话，在接下来的几年里出版它们。其内容如下：
进一步研究第一部分的主题。其他形式的命题(如“并非所有的x都是y”)。三字命题和多字命题(如“所有的a，b，c都是d，e”)。假设。进退两难。&amp；c.&amp；c.
把一个命题分析成它的要素。数值和几何问题。推理理论。问题的建构。和许多其他的古董罗吉卡(Curiosa Logica)。
自第一版以来，主要的修改是在“分类”一章(第2、3页)和“命题”一书(第10至19页)中进行的。主要增加的是在第94页的试卷中增加的单词和短语的问题，以及在第164页、194页插入的注释。
在第一册第二章中，我采用了“分类”的新定义，这使我能够将整个宇宙视为一个“类”，从而省去了“一套事物”这一非常尴尬的短语。
在“存在命题”一章中，我采用了一种新的“范式”，把肯定或否定存在的阶级看作命题的谓语，而不是主语，从而回避了困扰另一种形式的一个非常微妙的困难。这些微妙的困难似乎存在于每一棵知识树的根部，它们比它更高的树枝上出现的任何困难都更令人望而却步。例如，欧几里得第四十七号命题的困难，与在努力想出直线的本质时所遭受的精神折磨相比，只是孩子们的游戏。而且，在目前的工作中，与令人困惑的问题“什么是东西”相比，第192页的“5个骗子”问题的困难是“琐碎的，轻如空气的”。
在“关系命题”一章中，我插入了一个新的章节，其中包含一个命题，以“全部”开头，是一个双重命题的证明(这一事实与下一节规定的任意性规则相当独立，即这样一个命题应被理解为暗示其主体的实际存在)。这个证明是在较早的版本中，顺便提一下，在讨论双文图的过程中给出的：但它在这篇论文中的适当位置，是我现在介绍它的地方。
在索利特的例子中，我做了很多语言上的改动，以逃避一个困难，我担心这会让前三个版本的一些读者感到困惑。有些前提的措辞不符合大学的规定。在词典中命名的，但属于一个更大的班级，其中大学。只是其中的一部分。在所有这些情况下，其意图是读者应该意识到，大阶级的主张因此就是对大学的主张，而读者应该忽略它对其他部分的主张，认为这是多余的。因此，在Ex.。15，大学。第三个前言是“邦德夫人没有灰鸭子”，也就是“这个村子里的鸭子”，也就是“邦德夫人没有灰鸭子”。“没有一只灰鸭是邦德太太的鸭子。”这里的术语不是大学的物种，而是更大的一类“鸭”，大学就是其中的一类。这只是其中的一部分：读者应该意识到，这里所说的“鸭子”就是“这个村子里的鸭子”。他应该把这个前提当作“邦德太太在这个村子里没有灰鸭子”来对待，并且应该忽略它对班上其他“鸭子”，即“鸭子”的断言，认为这是多余的。“邦德太太没有灰鸭离开这个村子。”
在附录中，我对“五个说谎者”的问题给出了一个新的版本。我这样做的目的是为了逃避微妙而神秘的困难，这些困难困扰着所有试图将一个命题视为自己的主题，或将一组命题视为彼此的主题。当然，这是一种最令人困惑和不令人满意的理论：人们不禁感到，在所有这些神秘的东道主中，都非常缺乏实质内容--当幽灵的队伍在我们面前滑行时，没有一个可以让我们扑过去说：“这里有一个命题，要么是真的，要么是假的！”--它只是一个我们被召唤去参加的Barmecide Feast--它的原型是在那个神秘的岛屿上找到的，那里的居民“赚了一杯”。通过简单地将“说2个真相”翻译成“两种调味品(盐和芥末)”，“说2个谎言”翻译成“两种调味品都不吃”，“说一个真相和一个谎言(未指明顺序)”到“只吃一种调味品(没有具体说明哪种调味品)”，我就摆脱了所有那些形而上学的困惑，并产生了一个问题，当翻译成一组符号化的前提时，提供的数据与“五个说谎者”问题提供的数据完全相同。“五个说谎者”的问题提供的数据与“五个说谎者”问题提供的数据完全相同。“五个说谎者”的问题提供的数据与“五个说谎者”问题提供的数据完全相同。

最后，让我指出，即使是那些为了能够回答该科目的试卷而有义务学习形式逻辑的人，也会发现符号逻辑的研究对这一目的最有帮助，因为它揭示了形式逻辑充斥着的许多晦涩之处，并提供了一种令人愉快的简单方法来检验由形式逻辑强加给其选民的繁琐过程所得出的结果。
我相信，这是第一次(除了我自己在1886年出版的一本小书“逻辑的游戏”(The Game Of Logic)，这是一次非常不完整的表演)，是为了普及这一迷人的主题。这让我付出了多年的辛勤劳动：但是，如果事实证明，正如我希望的那样，它能够真正服务于年轻人，并在高中和私人家庭中被作为他们健康的精神娱乐存量的宝贵补充，那么这样的结果将偿还我在这上面花费的劳动的十倍以上。
[注：一些致予教师的备注载于附录第165页。]。
如果学习者希望公平地回答这个问题，即这本小书是否为最有趣的精神娱乐提供了素材，我们诚挚地建议他们采用以下规则：
(1)从头开始，不要让自己随意翻阅这本书来满足自己的好奇心。(1)从头开始，不要让自己到处翻阅这本书来满足自己的好奇心。这很可能会导致你把它扔在一边，说“这对我来说太难了！”，从而失去了在你的精神愉悦中增加一个非常大的项目的机会。这条规则(不要浸泡)对于其他类型的书是非常可取的--比如小说，在这些书中，你可能很容易破坏你从故事中获得的许多乐趣，因为深入了解它，你就会理所当然地得到作者所说的令人愉快的惊喜。我知道，有些人习惯于调查沃尔。首先，我想看看故事的结局：也许只要知道这对饱受迫害的恋人终究结婚了，他被证明是无辜的，邪恶的表弟在他的阴谋中被完全挫败，并受到他应得的惩罚，以及印度的富有的叔叔(曲)也是如此，也许还好知道一切都是圆满结束的。为什么在印度？答案是。因为，不知何故，叔叔们永远不可能在其他地方致富)在恰到好处的时刻去世--在不厌其烦地读“第一卷”之前。我说，这只是一部小说允许的，在小说中。即使对于那些没有读过故事前半部分的人来说，三世也是有意义的；但是，对于一本科学书籍来说，这完全是一种疯狂：如果你在按常规路线到达之前读到后半部分，你会发现后半部分令人费解得无可救药。
(2)在你确定你已经完全理解了整本书，并且你已经正确地做了大部分(如果不是全部)已经设定的例子之前，不要开始任何新的章节或章节。(2)直到你确定你已经完全理解了整本书，并且你已经正确地做了大部分(如果不是全部的话)。只要你们意识到你们走过的这片土地都是绝对征服的，你们没有留下任何悬而未决的困难，这些困难以后一定会再出现的，你们的胜利前进就是容易的，是令人高兴的。否则，你会发现你的困惑状态随着你的前进而变得越来越糟，直到你在完全厌恶的情况下放弃这一切。
(3)当你读到任何你不理解的段落时，再读一遍：如果你仍然不理解，再读一遍：如果你失败了，即使读了三遍，很可能你的大脑也有点累了。那样的话，把书收起来，去做别的工作，第二天，当你新鲜地来到它的时候，你很可能会发现这是相当容易的。
(4)如果可能的话，找一些和蔼可亲的朋友，他会和你一起读这本书，和你一起讨论困难。交谈是克服困难的绝妙办法。当我遇到任何事情--无论是在逻辑学上还是在任何其他难学的学科上--完全让我迷惑不解，我发现即使在我独自一人的时候，大声地谈论它也是一种最基本的计划。一个人可以对自己如此清楚地解释事情！然后，你知道，一个人对自己非常有耐心：一个人从来不会对自己的愚蠢感到恼火！
亲爱的读者，如果你能忠实地遵守这些规则，并给我的小书一个真正公平的审判，我非常自信地向你保证，你会发现符号逻辑即使不是最吸引人的精神娱乐，也会是最吸引人的娱乐之一！在这第一部分中，我小心翼翼地避开了在我看来是一个十二四岁聪明的孩子所不能掌握的一切困难。我自己教给了许多孩子它的大部分内容，万岁，并让PG_xiiifound他们对这门学科产生了真正的智慧兴趣。对于那些成功地掌握了第一部分，并且像奥利弗一样开始“要求更多”的人，我希望在第二部分中提供一些可以容忍的坚硬的坚果来敲碎-这些坚果将需要他们恰好拥有的所有坚果夹子！
精神娱乐是我们所有人都需要的精神健康的东西；毫无疑问，你可以从游戏中获得很多健康的享受，比如五子棋、国际象棋和新的游戏“Halma”。但是，毕竟，当你在任何一届奥运会上让自己成为一流选手时，你就没有什么真正的东西可以展示了！你享受这场比赛，毫无疑问，当时的胜利：但你没有可以珍惜的结果，并从中获得真正的好处。而且，一直以来，你都没有开发出一座完美的财富宝库。一旦掌握了符号逻辑的机器，你手头就会有一项精神上的工作，那就是吸引人们的兴趣，这项工作对你所学的任何学科都会有真正的用处。它会让你思路清晰--有能力把你的想法安排得井井有条，一针见血--而且，更有价值的是，你有能力发现谬误，粉碎那些你在书上、报纸上、演讲中、甚至在布道中经常遇到的脆弱的不合逻辑的论点，这些论点很容易迷惑那些从未不辞辛劳地掌握这门迷人艺术的人。这是一种非常有价值的能力，可以帮助你发现谬误，粉碎那些你会在书中、报纸上、演讲中、甚至在布道中不断遇到的脆弱的不合逻辑的论点，而这些论点很容易迷惑那些从未不辞辛劳地掌握这门迷人艺术的人。试试看。这就是我对你的全部要求！

[例如，“大”、“红”、“旧”，“我昨天收到的。”]。
[因此，事物“一朵玫瑰”可以具有“红色”、“有香味”、“成熟”等属性，而属性“红色”可以属于事物“一朵玫瑰”、“一块砖”、“一条丝带”和“一条丝带”。
[引入这个词是为了避免短语“属性或属性集”的不断重复。
因此，我们可以说一朵玫瑰有“红”的属性(或附属的“红”，随便我们喜欢)；或者我们可以说它的附属的“红色，芬芳和盛开”。]。
“分类”，或称类的形成，是一个心理过程，在这个过程中，我们想象自己把某些东西放在一个组中。这样的群体被称为“班级”。
(1)我们可以想象我们已经把所有的东西都放在一起了。这样形成的类(即类“物”)包含整个宇宙。
(2)我们可以想到类“物”，并且可以想象，我们已经从它中挑出了所有具有某种附加物的东西，而这些东西不是整个类所具有的。据说这是这样组成的班级所特有的。在这种情况下，类“物”相对于这样形成的类被称为“属”：这样形成的类被称为类“物”的“种”，其特殊的附加物被称为它的“差别”。
因为这个过程完全是心智的，我们可以执行它，不管有没有现有的东西拥有那个附属物。如果有，就说这个类是“真实的”；如果没有，就说它是“不真实的”，或者说是“想象的”。
例如，我们可以设想，我们已经从阶级“物”中挑出了所有具有附属的“物质的、人造的、由房屋和街道组成的东西”；这样，我们就可以形成真正的阶级“镇”。在这里，我们可以把“物”看作一个属，把“镇”看作一种东西，把“物质的、人造的、由房屋和街道组成的”作为它的区别。
再一次，我们可以想象，我们已经挑选出了所有拥有附属品的东西，“重达一吨，婴儿容易举起”；因此，我们可以形成想象的类别，“重达一吨，容易被婴儿举起的东西”。
(3)我们可以想到某个阶级，而不是这个阶级的“东西”，并且可以想象，我们已经从这个阶级中挑出了它的所有成员，它们拥有一个不为整个阶级所拥有的附属物。(3)我们可以想到某个阶级，而不是这个阶级的“东西”。据说这是这样组成的较小班级所特有的。在这种情况下，所认为的阶级相对于从它中挑选出来的较小的阶级被称为“属”：较小的阶级被称为较大的阶级的“物种”，其特殊的附属物被称为它的“差别”。
例如，我们可以把“城镇”这一类想像出来，把所有具有“燃气照明”属性的城镇都挑出来，这样我们就可以形成真正的“燃气城镇”。在这里，我们可以把“镇”看作一个属，把“燃气镇”看作城镇的一种，把“燃气灯”作为它的区别。
如果在上面的例子中，我们把“燃气灯”改成“黄金铺成的”，我们就会得到想象中的“黄金铺成的城镇”。]
例如，“有四百万居民的城镇”类，这个类只有一个成员，即。“伦敦。”]。
因此，任何一个我们可以命名以区别于所有其他事物的事物，都可以被认为是一个一人班。
[因此，“伦敦”可以被认为是从“城镇”阶级中挑选出来的一人阶级，“城镇”阶级的不同之处在于“有四百万居民”。]。
包含两个或多个成员的类有时被视为一个单独的事物。如被视为附属物，则该附属物可拥有并非其任何单独取得的成员所拥有的附加物。
[因此，“十团兵”这一类，当被视为一个单一的事物时，可能具有“成正方形”的属性，而这是它的任何成员都不具备的。]。
“划分”是一种心理过程，在这个过程中，我们会想到某一类东西，并想象我们把它分成了两个或两个以上的小类。
[因此，我们可能会想到“图书”这一类，并设想我们已将其分为两个较小的类别“装订图书”和“未装订图书”，或分为三个类别：“定价低于一先令的图书”、“先令图书”、“定价超过一先令的图书”，或分为二十六个类别，即“名称以A开头的图书”、“名称以B开头的图书，”&amp；c。]。
由某一师获得的班级被称为与该师所获得的每个班级“同科”的班级。
[因此，“装订书”类与“装订书”和“未装订书”这两个类别中的每一个类别是并列的。
同样，滑铁卢战役可以说与1815年发生的每一次事件都是“同时代的”。]。
同样，滑铁卢战役也可以说是“当代”的。]。
如果我们想一想某个班级，想象我们从中挑选了一个小班级，很明显，剩下的大班级没有那个小班级的差别。因此，它可以被认为是另一个较小的类，其差异可能是形成的，从那些第一次挑选的类，通过前缀“不”；我们可以想象，我们已经把我们首先想到的类分为两个较小的类，它们的差异是矛盾的。这种划分被称为“二分法”。
[例如，我们可以将“图书”分为“旧”和“不旧”两类。]。
在执行这一过程中，我们有时可能会发现，我们选择的属性在日常对话中使用得如此松散，以至于很难决定哪些东西属于一个类别，哪些属于另一个类别。在这种情况下，有必要制定一些武断的规则，规定一个班级应该从哪里结束，另一个班级应该从哪里开始。
因此，把“书”分成“旧的”和“不旧的”，我们可以说：“让所有的书在公元前印刷。1801年，被视为‘老’，所有其他被视为‘不老’。“]。

                        An envelope, containing two blank Diagrams (Biliteral and Triliteral)and 9 counters (4 Red and 5 Grey), may be had, from Messrs. Macmillan,for 3 d., by post 4 d.
    I shall be grateful to any Reader of this book who will point out anymistakes or misprints he may happen to notice in it, or any passagewhich he thinks is not clearly expressed.
    I have a quantity of MS. in hand for Parts II and III, and hope to beable——should life, and health, and opportunity, be granted to me, topublish them in the course of the next few years. Their contents will beas follows:—
    Further investigations in the subjects of Part I. Propositions of otherforms (such as “Not-all  x are  y”). Triliteral and MultiliteralPropositions (such as “All  a b c are  d e”). Hypotheticals. Dilemmas. &c.&c.
    Analysis of a Proposition into its Elements. Numerical and GeometricalProblems. The Theory of Inference. The Construction of Problems. Andmany other  Curiosa Logica.
    The chief alterations, since the First Edition, have been made in theChapter on ‘Classification’ ( pp. 2,  3) and the Book on ‘Propositions’( pp. 10 to  19). The chief additions have been the questions on words andphrases, added to the Examination-Papers at  p. 94, and the Notesinserted at  pp. 164,   194.
  In Book I, Chapter II, I have adopted a new definition of‘Classification’, which enables me to regard the whole Universe as a‘Class,’ and thus to dispense with the very awkward phrase ‘a Set ofThings.’
  In the Chapter on ‘Propositions of Existence’ I have adopted a new‘normal form,’ in which the Class, whose existence is affirmed ordenied, is regarded as the  Predicate, instead of the  Subject, of theProposition, thus evading a very subtle difficulty which besets theother form. These subtle difficulties seem to lie at the root of everyTree of Knowledge, and they are  far more hopeless to grapple with thanany that occur in its higher branches. For example, the difficulties ofthe Forty-Seventh Proposition of Euclid are mere child’s play comparedwith the mental torture endured in the effort to think out the essentialnature of a straight Line. And, in the present work, the difficulties ofthe “5 Liars” Problem, at  p.  192, are “trifles, light as air,” comparedwith the bewildering question “What is a Thing?”
  In the Chapter on ‘Propositions of Relation’ I have inserted a newSection, containing the proof that a Proposition, beginning with “All,”is a  Double Proposition (a fact that is quite independent of thearbitrary rule, laid down in the next Section, that such a Propositionis to be understood as implying the actual  existence of its Subject).This proof was given, in the earlier editions, incidentally, in thecourse of the discussion of the Biliteral Diagram: but its  properplace, in this treatise, is where I have now introduced it.
  pg_ixIn the Sorites-Examples, I have made a good many verbal alterations, inorder to evade a difficulty, which I fear will have perplexed some ofthe Readers of the first three Editions. Some of the Premisses were soworded that their Terms were not Specieses of the Univ. named in theDictionary, but of a larger Class, of which the Univ. was only aportion. In all such cases, it was intended that the Reader shouldperceive that what was asserted of the larger Class was thereby assertedof the Univ., and should ignore, as superfluous, all that it asserted ofits  other portion. Thus, in Ex. 15, the Univ. was stated to be “ducksin this village,” and the third Premiss was “Mrs. Bond has no grayducks,” i.e. “No gray ducks are ducks belonging to Mrs. Bond.” Here theTerms are  not Specieses of the Univ., but of the larger Class “ducks,”of which the Univ. is only a portion: and it was intended that theReader should perceive that what is here asserted of “ ducks” is therebyasserted of “ ducks in this village.” and should treat this Premiss as ifit were “Mrs. Bond has no gray ducks in this village,” and shouldignore, as superfluous, what it asserts as to the  other portion of theClass “ducks,” viz. “ Mrs. Bond has no gray ducks  out of this village”.
  In the Appendix I have given a new version of the Problem of the “FiveLiars.” My object, in doing so, is to escape the subtle and mysteriousdifficulties which beset all attempts at regarding a Proposition asbeing its own Subject, or a Set of Propositions as being Subjects forone another. It is certainly, a most bewildering and unsatisfactorytheory: one cannot help feeling that there is a great lack of substance in all this shadowy host——that, as the procession ofphantoms glides before us, there is not  one that we can pounce upon,and say “ Here is a Proposition that  must be either true orfalse!”——that it is but a Barmecide Feast, to which we have beenbidden——and that its prototype is to be found in that mythical island,whose inhabitants “earned a precarious living by taking in each others’washing”! By simply translating “telling 2 Truths” into “taking  bothof 2 condiments (salt and mustard),” “telling 2 Lies” into “taking neither of them” and “telling a Truth and a Lie (order not specified)”into “taking only  one condiment (it is not specified   pg_x which),” I haveescaped all those metaphysical puzzles, and have produced a Problemwhich, when translated into a Set of symbolized Premisses, furnishes thevery same  Data as were furnished by the Problem of the “Five Liars.”
  The coined words, introduced in previous editions, such as “Eliminands”and “Retinends”, perhaps hardly need any apology: they wereindispensable to my system: but the new plural, here used for the firsttime, viz. “Soriteses”, will, I fear, be condemned as “bad English”,unless I say a word in its defence. We have  three singular nouns, inEnglish, of plural  form, “series”, “species”, and “Sorites”: in allthree, the awkwardness, of using the same word for both singular andplural, must often have been felt: this has been remedied, in the caseof “series” by coining the plural “serieses”, which has already foundit way into the dictionaries: so I am no rash innovator, but am merely“following suit”, in using the new plural “Soriteses”.
  In conclusion, let me point out that even those, who are obliged tostudy  Formal Logic, with a view to being able to answerExamination-Papers in that subject, will find the study of  SymbolicLogic most helpful for this purpose, in throwing light upon many of theobscurities with which Formal Logic abounds, and in furnishing adelightfully easy method of  testing the results arrived at by thecumbrous processes which Formal Logic enforces upon its votaries.
  This is, I believe, the very first attempt (with the exception of my ownlittle book,  The Game of Logic, published in 1886, a very incompleteperformance) that has been made to  popularise this fascinatingsubject. It has cost me  years of hard work: but if it should prove, asI hope it may, to be of  real service to the young, and to be taken up,in High Schools and in private families, as a valuable addition to theirstock of healthful mental recreations, such a result would more thanrepay ten times the labour that I have expended on it.
          [N.B. Some remarks, addressed to  Teachers, will be found in theAppendix, at  p. 165.]
  The Learner, who wishes to try the question  fairly, whether thislittle book does, or does not, supply the materials for a mostinteresting mental recreation, is  earnestly advised to adopt thefollowing Rules:—
  (1) Begin at the  beginning, and do not allow yourself to gratify amere idle curiosity by dipping into the book, here and there. This wouldvery likely lead to your throwing it aside, with the remark “This is much too hard for me!”, and thus losing the chance of adding a very large item to your stock of mental delights. This Rule (of not dipping) is very  desirable with  other kinds of books——such asnovels, for instance, where you may easily spoil much of the enjoymentyou would otherwise get from the story, by dipping into it further on,so that what the author meant to be a pleasant surprise comes to you asa matter of course. Some people, I know, make a practice of looking intoVol. III first, just to see how the story ends: and perhaps it  is aswell just to know that all ends  happily——that the much-persecutedlovers  do marry after all, that he is proved to be quite innocent ofthe murder, that the wicked cousin is completely foiled in his plot andgets the punishment he deserves, and that the rich uncle in India ( Qu.Why in  India?  Ans. Because, somehow, uncles never  can get richanywhere else) dies at exactly the right moment——before taking thetrouble to read Vol. I.   pg_xiiThis, I say, is  just permissible with a novel, where Vol. III has a  meaning, even for those who have notread the earlier part of the story; but, with a  scientific book, it issheer insanity: you will find the latter part  hopelesslyunintelligible, if you read it before reaching it in regular course.
  (2) Don’t begin any fresh Chapter, or Section, until you are certainthat you  thoroughly understand the whole book  up to that point, andthat you have worked, correctly, most if not all of the examples whichhave been set. So long as you are conscious that all the land you havepassed through is absolutely  conquered, and that you are leaving nounsolved difficulties  behind you, which will be sure to turn up againlater on, your triumphal progress will be easy and delightful.Otherwise, you will find your state of puzzlement get worse and worse asyou proceed, till you give up the whole thing in utter disgust.
  (3) When you come to any passage you don’t understand,  read it again:if you  still don’t understand it,  read it again: if you fail, evenafter  three readings, very likely your brain is getting a littletired. In that case, put the book away, and take to other occupations,and next day, when you come to it fresh, you will very likely find thatit is  quite easy.
  (4) If possible, find some genial friend, who will read the book alongwith you, and will talk over the difficulties with you.  Talking is awonderful smoother-over of difficulties. When  I come uponanything——in Logic or in any other hard subject——that entirelypuzzles me, I find it a capital plan to talk it over,  aloud, even whenI am all alone. One can explain things so  clearly to one’s self! Andthen, you know, one is so  patient with one’s self: one  never getsirritated at one’s own stupidity!
  If, dear Reader, you will faithfully observe these Rules, and so give mylittle book a really  fair trial, I promise you, most confidently, thatyou will find Symbolic Logic to be one of the most, if not  the most,fascinating of mental recreations! In this First Part, I have carefullyavoided all difficulties which seemed to me to be beyond the grasp of anintelligent child of (say) twelve or fourteen years of age. I havemyself taught most of its contents,  vivâ voce, to  many children, andhave   pg_xiiifound them take a real intelligent interest in the subject. Forthose, who succeed in mastering Part I, and who begin, like Oliver,“asking for more,” I hope to provide, in Part II, some  tolerably hardnuts to crack——nuts that will require all the nut-crackers they happento possess!
  Mental recreation is a thing that we all of us need for our mentalhealth; and you may get much healthy enjoyment, no doubt, from Games,such as Back-gammon, Chess, and the new Game “Halma”. But, after all,when you have made yourself a first-rate player at any one of theseGames, you have nothing real to  show for it, as a  result! Youenjoyed the Game, and the victory, no doubt,  at the time: but you haveno  result that you can treasure up and get real  good out of. And,all the while, you have been leaving unexplored a perfect  mine ofwealth. Once master the machinery of Symbolic Logic, and you have amental occupation always at hand, of absorbing interest, and one thatwill be of real  use to you in  any subject you may take up. It willgive you clearness of thought——the ability to  see your way through apuzzle——the habit of arranging your ideas in an orderly andget-at-able form——and, more valuable than all, the power to detect fallacies, and to tear to pieces the flimsy illogical arguments, whichyou will so continually encounter in books, in newspapers, in speeches,and even in sermons, and which so easily delude those who have nevertaken the trouble to master this fascinating Art.  Try it. That is allI ask of you!

    [Thus, the Thing “a rose” may have the Attributes “red,”“scented,” “full-blown,” &c.; and the Attribute “red” may belongto the Things “a rose,” “a brick,” “a ribbon,” &c.]
    [This word is introduced in order to avoid the constantrepetition of the phrase “Attribute or Set of Attributes.”
  Thus, we may say that a rose has the Attribute “red” (or theAdjunct “red,” whichever we prefer); or we may say that it hasthe Adjunct “red, scented and full-blown.”]
      ‘Classification,’ or the formation of Classes, is a Mental Process, inwhich we imagine that we have put together, in a group, certain Things. Such a group is called a ‘ Class.’
    (1) We may imagine that we have put together all Things. The Class soformed (i.e. the Class “Things”) contains the whole Universe.
  (2) We may think of the Class “Things,” and may imagine that we havepicked out from it all the Things which possess a certain Adjunct  notpossessed by the whole Class.  This Adjunct is said to be ‘ peculiar’ tothe Class so formed.  In this case, the Class “Things” is called a‘ Genus’ with regard to the Class so formed:  the Class, so formed, iscalled a ‘ Species’ of the Class “Things”:  and its peculiar Adjunct iscalled its ‘ Differentia’.
  pg002As this Process is entirely  Mental, we can perform it whether there is, or  is not, an  existing Thing which possesses that Adjunct.  Ifthere  is, the Class is said to be ‘ Real’; if not, it is said to be‘ Unreal’, or ‘ Imaginary.’
  [For example, we may imagine that we have picked out, from theClass “Things,” all the Things which possess the Adjunct“material, artificial, consisting of houses and streets”; and wemay thus form the Real Class “towns.” Here we may regard“Things” as a  Genus, “Towns” as a  Species of Things, and“material, artificial, consisting of houses and streets” as its Differentia.
  Again, we may imagine that we have picked out all the Thingswhich possess the Adjunct “weighing a ton, easily lifted by ababy”; and we may thus form the  Imaginary Class “Things thatweigh a ton and are easily lifted by a baby.”]
  (3) We may think of a certain Class,  not the Class “Things,” and mayimagine that we have picked out from it all the Members of it whichpossess a certain Adjunct  not possessed by the whole Class. ThisAdjunct is said to be ‘ peculiar’ to the smaller Class so formed. Inthis case, the Class thought of is called a ‘ Genus’ with regard to thesmaller Class picked out from it: the smaller Class is called a‘ Species’ of the larger: and its peculiar Adjunct is called its‘ Differentia’.
  [For example, we may think of the Class “towns,” andimagine that we have picked out from it all the towns whichpossess the Attribute “lit with gas”; and we may thus formthe Real Class “towns lit with gas.” Here we may regard“Towns” as a  Genus, “Towns lit with gas” as a  Species ofTowns, and “lit with gas” as its  Differentia.
  If, in the above example, we were to alter “lit with gas” into“paved with gold,” we should get the  Imaginary Class “townspaved with gold.”]
    [For example, the Class “towns having four million inhabitants,”which Class contains only  one Member, viz. “London.”]
  pg002½ Hence, any single Thing, which we can name so as to distinguish it fromall other Things, may be regarded as a one-Member Class.
  [Thus “London” may be regarded as the one-Member Class, pickedout from the Class “towns,” which has, as its Differentia,“having four million inhabitants.”]
  A Class, containing two or more Members, is sometimes regarded as  onesingle Thing. When so regarded, it may possess an Adjunct which is not possessed by any Member of it taken separately.
  [Thus, the Class “The soldiers of the Tenth Regiment,” whenregarded as  one single Thing, may possess the Attribute“formed in square,” which is  not possessed by any Member of ittaken separately.]
          ‘Division’ is a Mental Process, in which we think of a certain Class ofThings, and imagine that we have divided it into two or more smallerClasses.
  [Thus, we might think of the Class “books,” and imagine that wehad divided it into the two smaller Classes “bound books” and“unbound books,” or into the three Classes, “books priced atless than a shilling,” “shilling-books,” “books priced at morethan a shilling,” or into the twenty-six Classes, “books whosenames begin with  A,” “books whose names begin with  B,” &c.]
  A Class, that has been obtained by a certain Division, is said to be‘codivisional’ with every Class obtained by that Division.
  [Thus, the Class “bound books” is codivisional with each of thetwo Classes, “bound books” and “unbound books.”
  Similarly, the Battle of Waterloo may be said to have been“contemporary” with every event that happened in 1815.]
    Similarly, the Battle of Waterloo may be said to have been“contemporary” with itself.]
      If we think of a certain Class, and imagine that we have picked out fromit a certain smaller Class, it is evident that the  Remainder of thelarge Class does  not possess the Differentia of that smaller Class.Hence it may be regarded as  another smaller Class, whose Differentiamay be formed, from that of the Class first picked out, by prefixing theword “not”; and we may imagine that we have  divided the Class firstthought of into  two smaller Classes, whose Differentiæ are contradictory. This kind of Division is called ‘ Dichotomy’.
  [For example, we may divide “books” into the two Classes whoseDifferentiæ are “old” and “not-old.”]
  In performing this Process, we may sometimes find that the Attributes wehave chosen are used so loosely, in ordinary conversation, that it isnot easy to decide  which of the Things belong to the one Class and which to the other. In such a case, it would be necessary to lay downsome arbitrary  rule, as to  where the one Class should end and theother begin.

我很高兴宣布SWIFT服务器生态系统的一个新开源项目-SWIFT AWS Lambda Runtime。SWIFT AWS Lambda Runtime作为SWIFT软件包分发，旨在帮助SWIFT开发人员为Amazon Web Services Lambda平台构建无服务器功能。
该项目是一个团队的努力，包括SWIFT社区的工程师，包括来自苹果和亚马逊的工程师。值得注意的是，法比安·费特是社区工作的先驱，也是图书馆的合著者。作为一个开源库，任何有兴趣为该项目做出贡献的人都可以很容易地加入进来，帮助它变得更好。
许多现代系统都有客户端组件，如iOS、MacOS或WatchOS应用程序，以及这些客户端与之交互的服务器组件。对于客户端应用程序开发人员来说，无服务器功能通常是将其应用程序扩展到云中的最简单、最有效的方式。
无服务器功能正成为在云中运行事件驱动或临时计算任务的日益流行的选择。它们为任务关键型微服务和数据密集型工作负载提供支持。在许多情况下，考虑到开发人员的按需特性，无服务器功能允许开发人员更轻松地扩展和控制计算成本。
在使用无服务器功能时，必须注意资源利用率，因为它直接影响系统成本。这就是斯威夫特大放异彩的地方！凭借其低内存占用、确定性性能和快速启动时间，SWIFT是无服务器功能架构的绝佳匹配。
将这一点与SWIFT对开发人员的友好性、表现力和对安全性的重视相结合，我们就拥有了一个适合所有技能水平、可扩展且经济高效的开发人员的解决方案。
SWIFT AWS Lambda Runtime旨在使在SWIFT中构建Lambda函数变得简单而安全。该库是AWS Lambda Runtime API的实现，并使用嵌入式异步HTTP客户端，该客户端针对AWS Runtime环境中的性能进行了微调。该库提供了一个多层API，允许构建一系列Lambda函数：从快速而简单的闭包到复杂的、性能敏感的事件处理程序。
使用AWS Lambda Runtime的最简单方式是传入闭包，例如：
//导入模块import AWSLambdaRuntime//在本例中，我们接收并响应字符串Lambda。在callback(.)中运行{(context，payload：string，callback)。成功(&#34；Hello，\(有效负载)&#34；)}。
更常见的情况是，有效负载将是JSON，它是使用COODABLE建模的，例如：
//导入模块导入AWSLambdaRuntime//请求，使用codable进行透明JSON编码私有结构请求：codable{let name：string}//响应，使用codable进行透明JSON编码私有结构响应：codable{let message：string}//在本例中我们使用codable Lambda接收和响应JSON。在callback(.)中运行{(context，request：request，callback)。Success(响应(Message：&#34；Hello，\(Request.。姓名)&#34；)}。
由于Lambda函数通常由源自AWS平台的事件(如SNS、SQS或S3事件)触发，因此该软件包还包括AWSLambdaEvents模块，提供这些常见触发事件类型的实现。例如，处理SQS消息：
//导入模块导入AWSLambdaRuntime import AWSLambdaEvents//在本例中，我们收到一条SQS消息，没有响应(Void)Lambda。运行{(上下文，消息：SQ.。留言、回叫)在.。回调(.。Success(void()}。
除了这些常见的触发事件，AWSLambdaEvents还包括用于将Lambda函数与APIGateway集成的抽象，APIGateway是一个AWS系统，可帮助将Lambda函数公开为HTTP端点。
//导入模块导入AWSLambdaRuntime import AWSLambdaEvents//在此示例中，我们收到一个APIGateway.V2.Request，//并使用APIGateway.V2.Response Lambda进行重新发布。运行{(上下文，请求：APIGateway。V2。请求、回叫)在.。回调(.。成功(APIGateway。V2。响应(statusCode：。接受)}。
将Lambda函数建模为闭包既简单又安全。SWIFT AWS Lambda Runtime将确保将用户提供的功能从网络处理线程卸载到其自己的线程，以便即使代码变慢或没有响应，底层Lambda流程也可以继续并与运行时引擎交互。这种安全性是以网络线程和处理线程之间的上下文切换带来的小性能损失为代价的。在大多数情况下，使用基于闭包的API的简单性和安全性优于下面详述的面向性能的API的复杂性。
对性能敏感的Lambda函数可以选择使用更复杂的API，该API允许用户代码在与网络处理程序相同的线程上运行。SWIFT AWS Lambda Runtime使用SwiftNIO作为其底层网络引擎，这意味着这些API基于SwiftNIO的并发原语，如EventLoop和EventLoopFuture。
//导入模块导入AWSLambdaRuntime import AWSLambdaEvents import NIO//我们的Lambda处理程序，符合EventLoopLambdaHandler struct Handler：EventLoopLambdaHandler{typealias in=sns。message//请求类型类型别名out=void//响应类型，或void//在此示例中，我们接收的SNS消息没有响应(Void)函数句柄(上下文：lambda。上下文，有效负载：输入)-&gt；EventLoopFuture&lt；输出&gt；{.。上下文。eventLoop。Make SucceededFuture(void())}}Lambda。Run(Handler())。
除了使用基于EventLoopFuture的API的认知复杂性之外，请注意这些API的使用应该格外小心。EventLoopLambdaHandler将在与库的网络引擎相同的EventLoop(线程)上执行用户提供的函数，这对实现提出了永不阻塞底层EventLoop的要求。换句话说，Lambda代码不应该使用阻塞API调用，因为它可能会阻止库与Lambda平台交互。
这是一个社区驱动的开源项目积极寻求贡献的开始。虽然核心API被认为是稳定的，但API仍可能随着其接近1.0版本而发展。有几个领域需要额外关注，包括但不限于：
如果您对SWIFT AWS Lambda Runtime感兴趣，请来参与！源代码是可用的，我们鼓励来自开源社区的贡献。如果您有反馈、问题或想要讨论该项目，请随时在SWIFT论坛上聊天。如果您想报告错误，请使用GitHub问题跟踪器。我们期待着与您合作，并帮助推动行业迈向更好、更安全的编程未来。
请随时在SWIFT论坛的相关帖子上发布有关此帖子的问题。

周三，亚利桑那州总检察长马克·布诺维奇(Mark Brnovich)起诉谷歌(Google)，指控这家科技巨头在用户关闭数字跟踪的情况下收集用户下落的信息，侵犯了用户的隐私。
这起诉讼要求未指明的赔偿，理由是安卓智能手机操作系统制造商谷歌(Google)的移动软件设置方式丰富了其广告帝国，并在实际为设备用户提供的个人数据保护方面欺骗了设备所有者，违反了亚利桑那州禁止公司歪曲商业行为的消费者保护法。
Brnovich(R)在一次采访中说：“当消费者试图退出谷歌收集的位置数据时，该公司正在继续寻找误导性的方式来获取信息并将其用于盈利。”
谷歌发言人何塞·卡斯塔内达(Jose Castaneda)在一份声明中为公司的隐私做法辩护，强调国家及其“提起诉讼的应急费用律师似乎错误地描述了我们的服务。”
他说：“我们一直在我们的产品中内置隐私功能，并为位置数据提供强大的控制。我们期待着澄清事实，“他说。
此案标志着监管机构对谷歌隐私做法的最新谴责，十年来，这家科技巨头被指控错误跟踪用户的网络浏览行为，不当窃取学生数据，并非法收集在YouTube上观看卡通的儿童的详细信息。在美国，罚款总额高达数百万美元，但与使谷歌成为世界上最赚钱的公司之一的巨额年收入相比，这些罚款相形见绌。
然而，亚利桑那州的诉讼也反映了州和联邦监管机构越来越多的感觉，即谷歌已经变得太大、太强大和不受约束。其他州的总检察长和司法部的执法官员预计将在今年夏天对谷歌提出更多投诉，指控其业务的其他方面违反了反垄断法。
Brnovich说：“在某些时候，有很多钱的人或公司认为他们可以做他们想做的任何该死的事情，感觉他们凌驾于法律之上。”“我希望谷歌得到这样的信息，即亚利桑那州有一项州消费者欺诈法案。他们可能是世界上最具创新精神的公司，但这并不意味着他们可以凌驾于法律之上。“
在美联社(Associated Press)牵头对谷歌的跟踪行为进行调查后，亚利桑那州开始调查谷歌-这项调查最早是在2018年由《华盛顿邮报》(The Washington Post)报道的。
通常，运行安卓系统的智能手机允许用户关闭追踪其动作的功能。但该州在周三的起诉书中声称，这些设备仍然记录并保留了某些应用程序的位置记录-包括地图和天气-以及搜索，即使是对禁用位置跟踪的用户也是如此。
根据亚利桑那州的诉讼，如果用户想要阻止这条数字踪迹被记录下来，他们不得不关闭第二个难以找到的设置，该诉讼指责谷歌错综复杂的菜单具有欺骗性。该州的指控呼应了隐私专家和国会议员的观点，尽管谷歌此前坚称，它“对这些工具进行了清晰的描述，并提供了强有力的控制，以便人们可以随时打开或关闭这些工具，并删除他们的历史记录。”
这份经过大量编辑的文件似乎表明，谷歌可能在位置跟踪方面误导了用户的其他情况。亚利桑那州声称，菜单很难找到，在某些情况下，谷歌在“没有通知用户的情况下”改变了默认的跟踪设置，更不用说寻求或获得同意了。在这样做的过程中，州官员指责谷歌“不合作的行为，拖延战术，以及总体上没有遵守”司法部长对记录的要求。
据亚利桑那州估计，谷歌收集位置数据的“看似坚持不懈的努力”反过来帮助这家科技巨头向亚利桑那州的居民投放了有针对性的广告，这些居民可能一开始就没有同意这样的跟踪。为此，布尔诺维奇要求法院要求谷歌返还其被指控的不当行为的非法所得，总额可能高达数亿美元，司法部长说。州反欺诈法还允许亚利桑那州每次违规获得高达1万美元的赔偿。
“包括亚利桑那州在内的用户已经开始每天依赖谷歌的产品和服务，”该州的起诉书称。“与此同时，通过这些欺骗性和不公平的行为和做法，如果用户想要有意义地退出谷歌收集的位置信息，谷歌就算不是不可能，也是不切实际的。”
note_en:
Arizona Attorney General Mark Brnovich sued Google on Wednesday, alleging the tech giant violated its users’ privacy by collecting information about their whereabouts even if they had turned off such digital tracking.
 The lawsuit seeks unspecified damages in arguing that Google, the maker of the Android smartphone operating system, set up its mobile software in a way that enriched its advertising empire and deceived device owners about the protections actually afforded to their personal data, running afoul of Arizona consumer-protection laws that prohibit companies from misrepresenting their business practices.
 “When consumers try to opt out of Google’s collection of location data, the company is continuing to find misleading ways to obtain information and use it for profit,” Brnovich (R) said in an interview.
   Jose Castaneda, a spokesman for Google, defended the company’s privacy practices in a statement, stressing the state and its “contingency fee lawyers filing this lawsuit appear to have mischaracterized our services."
 “We have always built privacy features into our products and provided robust controls for location data. We look forward to setting the record straight,” he said.
  The case marks  regulators’ latest rebuke of Google for its approach to privacy, a decade of allegations that the tech behemoth  wrongly tracked users’ web browsing behavior, improperly siphoned students’ data and illegally collected  details about children who were viewing cartoons on YouTube. The fines have totaled into the millions of dollars in the United States, but they pale in comparison to the massive annual revenue that make Google one of the world’s most profitable companies.
   Yet Arizona’s lawsuit also reflects a growing sense among state and federal regulators that Google has grown too big, powerful and unchecked. Other state attorneys general — and law-enforcement officials at the Justice Department — are  expected to file additional complaints against Google this summer, alleging that other elements of its business violate antitrust law.
 “At some point, people or companies that have a lot of money think they can do whatever the hell they want to do, and feel like they are above the law,” Brnovich said. “I wanted Google to get the message that Arizona has a state consumer fraud act. They may be the most innovative company in the world, but that doesn’t mean they’re above the law.”
  Arizona began probing Google — an inquiry  first reported by The Washington Post in 2018 — following an investigation led by the Associated Press into Google’s tracking behavior.
   Generally, smartphones running Android allow users to turn off a feature that tracks their movements. But the state alleged in its complaint Wednesday that these devices still recorded and kept location records for certain apps — including mapping and weather — as well as searches, even for users who disabled location tracking.
 Users instead had to turn off a second, hard-to-find setting if they wanted to prevent this  digital trail from being recorded, according to Arizona’s lawsuit, which faulted Google’s maze of menus as deceptive. The state’s charges echo the views of privacy experts and congressional lawmakers, though Google previously maintained it offered “clear descriptions of these tools, and robust controls so people can turn them on or off, and delete their histories at any time.”
 The heavily redacted filing appears to suggest other instances in which Google may have misled users about location tracking. Menus were hard to find, and in some cases, Google changed the default tracking settings “without informing the user, much less seeking or obtaining consent,” Arizona contends. In doing so, state officials faulted Google for “uncooperative conduct, delay tactics, and general failure to comply” with the attorney general’s demands for records.
   By Arizona’s estimation, Google’s “seemingly relentless drive” to collect location data in turn helped the tech giant deliver targeted ads to Arizona residents who may not have consented to such tracking in the first place. To that end, Brnovich asked a court to require Google to pay back ill-gotten profits from its alleged misdeeds, totaling perhaps hundreds of millions of dollars, the attorney general said. State anti-fraud laws also allow Arizona to obtain up to $10,000 per violation.
 “Users, including in Arizona, have come to rely on Google’s products and services on a daily basis,” the state’s complaint contends. “At the same time, through these deceptive and unfair acts and practices, Google makes it impractical if not impossible for users to meaningfully opt-out of Google’s collection of location information, should the users seek to do so.”
title: 亚利桑那州起诉谷歌涉嫌侵犯Android用户位置隐私
title_en: Arizona sues Google for allegedly violating Android users' location privacy
